Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

Shared · Open records · 2 requests pending

CORA Requests Tracker

All open records requests in one place — who was asked, what was asked, what came back. Military housing numbers cross-referenced with public data.

Requests sent
2
awaiting response
Requests ready to send
2
drafted, not yet sent
Requests still needed
3
not yet drafted
Total days to statutory deadline (CORA)
3 days
then 7-day extension possible
Sent — awaiting response
1. El Paso County Assessor — full parcel database export sent Apr 7, 2026
To: [contact via agency website]
Requesting: APN, owner name, owner mailing address, property class, year built, sq footage, assessed value, exemption status, metro district membership, lat/lng. Format: CSV/Excel/shapefile.
Sent: Apr 7 | Statutory deadline: Apr 10 | Extension deadline: Apr 21 | Follow-up if no response: Apr 11
2. Colorado Springs Utilities — residential account data 2005–2025 sent Apr 7, 2026
To: CSU public records contact — csu.org
Requesting: (1) total active residential accounts per year, (2) new accounts opened per year, (3) accounts closed per year, (4) zero/near-zero usage accounts per year. Aggregate only — no customer-level data.
Sent: Apr 7 | Statutory deadline: Apr 10 | Extension deadline: Apr 21 | Follow-up if no response: Apr 11
Ready to send — drafted
3. Fort Carson FOIA — housing occupancy and BAH by ZIP draft complete
To: [contact via agency website] | Also: efoia.army.mil
Requesting: on-post housing units + occupancy annually 2010–2025; BAH recipients by ZIP code (most recent year); PCS moves in/out annually 2010–2025; dependents registered annually. Also covers Peterson SFB and Schriever SFB. Fee waiver requested.
Statutory: 20 business days | Est. response: Jun–Aug 2026 | Army FOIA routinely delayed — track carefully
Note: See "Military numbers — public" tab for numbers already available without this FOIA. File when ready but don't wait on it to proceed.
4. Colorado Attorney General — formal CORA complaint draft complete
To: [contact via agency website] | CC: [contact via agency website]; [contact via agency website]; [contact via agency website]
Formal complaint against El Paso County Sheriff's Office and Open Records Office. Six legal violations identified. Requests formal investigation, production of eviction notice records, December 27 footage, 1826 Wooten Road conveyance records, and billing accounting.
No statutory deadline to file — but sooner strengthens position | Attach full email chain Mar 29 – Apr 6, 2026
Still needed — not yet drafted
5. El Paso County Development Services — certificates of occupancy not drafted
To: [contact via agency website] | [contact via agency website]
Request: all certificates of occupancy issued by address and year, 2005–2025. Will show the real gap between homes permitted and homes legally occupied. Critical for the housing need calculation.
CORA — 3-day statutory response | Suggest sending week of Apr 13
6. El Paso County Finance — impact fees collected by development not drafted
To: El Paso County Finance Department
Request: all transportation, water, park, and school impact fees collected annually by development name or ZIP code, 2005–2025. Will show what taxpayers funded for developments that still have vacant parcels.
CORA — 3-day statutory response | Suggest sending with CO above
7. City of Colorado Springs — short-term rental licenses by ZIP not drafted
To: City of Colorado Springs — CORA or business license office
Request: all active and historical short-term rental (Airbnb/VRBO) licenses by address and ZIP code, 2018–2025. Will reveal investor-held units invisible to vacancy surveys.
CORA — 3-day statutory response | Also check: coloradosprings.gov licensing portal
Ongoing — responses received, follow-up needed
El Paso County CORA — Sheriff's Office / Open Records (LRMD 2025) disputed — incomplete
To: [contact via agency website] | Beth DeStefano [contact via agency website]
DeStefano declared "complete" Apr 6. Response sent disputing completion. Seven specific records still outstanding: eviction notice identity, Dec 27 footage, 1826 Wooten Road conveyance, authorizing orders. AG complaint filed. Madsen invoked 7-day extension Apr 6 — deadline Apr 15.
Extension deadline: Apr 15 | If not cured: file Application for Order to Show Cause — C.R.S. § 24-72-204(5)
Publicly available military numbers — no FOIA needed public sources
Sources: (1) Fort Carson February 2025 Fact Sheet — home.army.mil/carson. (2) Colorado Springs Gazette, September 2025 — "A look at military base employment in Colorado Springs and El Paso County." (3) US Air Force Academy FY2024 Economic Impact Report. (4) Wikipedia Fort Carson CDP — 2020 US Census count. All figures are published by the installations themselves or reported from official Army/Space Force statements.
Fort Carson
Total workforce (active duty + reservists + civilian + contractors) Army Feb 202532,410
Fort Carson — 3rd largest employer in Colorado Army fact sheetbehind State of CO (36.5k) and DIA (35k)
Annual economic impact Army fact sheet$2.55 billion/yr
Fort Carson CDP population — 2020 Census Census — undercounts17,693 on-post
ZIP codes served: 80902, 80913 USPS / Armycivilian census severely undercounts both
Peterson Space Force Base
Space Operations Command total workforce Space Force Mar 202515,000
Includes: active duty, reserve, National Guard, civilian official statementdoes not include Space Command personnel
ZIP code: 80914 USPSon-post population not in civilian census
US Air Force Academy
Total personnel — cadets + active duty + reservists + civilian USAFA FY202410,482
Location: Northgate area ZIP 80921 USPSadjacent to Flying Horse, Flying Horse North MDs
Schriever Space Force Base
Personnel estimate public estimate — no official figure found~3,000+ military + civilian
ZIP code: 80912 USPSmilitary ZIP — civilian census not applicable
Total El Paso County military community
Fort Carson workforce (published)32,410
Peterson Space Operations Command (published)15,000
US Air Force Academy (published)10,482
Schriever (estimated)~3,000
Total military community workforce~60,892 published/est.
Dependents (spouses + children — NOT in above figures) estimated~20,000–30,000 additional
Total military community including dependents estimated~80,000–90,000
As % of El Paso County population (~744k)~11–12%
The published workforce numbers (60,892) include civilian employees and contractors who live off-post and DO appear in census data. Active duty only — the figure that creates housing demand distinct from civilian demand — is not broken out in the published totals. That is what the FOIA seeks. For now, Fort Carson's 2020 Census on-post count of 17,693 is the only verified on-post residential population figure.
BAH impact on local rents — confirmed public statements
Military family quoted in published reporting (2023)"BAH does not cover actual cost for housing and utilities"
Result: demand pressure in 80906, 80911, 80916, 80925 ZIPsnear Fort Carson gates
SW El Paso County — only submarket with positive rent growth 2024+0.7% CBRE est.
Fort Carson ZIP 80913 census-recorded civilian pop.2,990 — severe undercount confirmed
What the FOIA adds that public numbers don't have
Active duty ONLY (separate from civilian/contractor) — annualFOIA needed
On-post housing units occupied vs vacant — annualFOIA needed
PCS moves in/out annually — real rotation countFOIA needed
BAH recipients by ZIP code — off-post demand by areaFOIA needed
Dependents registered at Fort Carson — annualFOIA needed
Year-by-year change in force size (2010–2025)FOIA needed
What you can use right now from public data
Fort Carson total workforce (published Feb 2025)32,410 use now
Peterson Space Operations Command (published)15,000 use now
USAFA total (FY2024 economic impact report)10,482 use now
Fort Carson CDP on-post population (2020 Census)17,693 use now
$2.55B annual economic impact (Army)use now
SW El Paso only submarket with positive rent growth 2024use now — CBRE
You have enough public data to make the core argument: a military community of 60,000+ workers representing 11–12% of county population is systematically invisible to every housing survey and planning model. The FOIA adds precision — but the argument stands without it.
All outstanding request deadlines at a glance
CORA — Assessor (sent Apr 7)Statutory: Apr 10 | Extension: Apr 21
CORA — CSU (sent Apr 7)
